synthesis_reason: Worker stalled at max_turns (tool-use 29) just before Step 5 — the GameContainer.jsx call to initDiscoveryQuestBridge(). Steps 1-4 fully landed: discoveryUnlocks.js created, onRegionDiscovered + _emitRegionDiscovered exported from discoverySystem.js, _emitRegionDiscovered call wired into WorldMapScene.revealNode, initDiscoveryQuestBridge + consumePendingDiscoveryUnlocks added to questSystem.js. Without the GameContainer call, the listener was never registered and discovery events would have been no-ops at runtime. Orchestrator added the missing 2-line wiring (1 import + 1 invocation) and verified build passes (12.01s, 333+ modules). Per saved memory feedback_receipt_recovery.md: code lands, receipt synthesized, recovery audit log produced, dispatch reviewer normally.

synthesis_evidence.discoveryUnlocks_module_created: src/renderer/game/data/discoveryUnlocks.js — 73 lines. Schema documented inline. Four entries: desert_foraging→desert_healer, copper_mine→bridge_collapse, salt_river→boat_puzzle (all pending:false), mountain_range→null pending:true with TODO note. Worker keyed on WORLD_LOCATIONS IDs (location ids), not regions.js IDs. The worker explicitly documented this design choice + the audit-mismatch implications inside the file.
synthesis_evidence.discoverySystem_pubsub_added: src/renderer/game/systems/discoverySystem.js — onRegionDiscovered(callback) and _emitRegionDiscovered(regionId) added. Listener registry via module-private array with try/catch around dispatch. Returns unsubscribe closure.
synthesis_evidence.WorldMapScene_emit_wired: WorldMapScene.revealNode now calls _emitRegionDiscovered(nodeId) after revealArea + saveGame + redrawFog. Comment notes the keying decision (passing locationId, not loc.region) so the lookup in DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS works.
synthesis_evidence.questSystem_bridge_designed: src/renderer/game/systems/questSystem.js — exports initDiscoveryQuestBridge() and consumePendingDiscoveryUnlocks(state). The bridge uses a module-private _pendingDiscoveryUnlocks queue rather than direct startQuest invocations. This is a well-considered design choice: discovery events fire mid-scene-render which is not always the right moment to interrupt with a new quest. Scenes consume the queue at natural interaction checkpoints (NPC dialog, scene transition). Idempotent against duplicate startQuest calls (handled by startQuest itself).
synthesis_evidence.GameContainer_boot_wired_by_orchestrator: Two-line addition by orchestrator (mirrors the existing runRuntimeAudit pattern): 1 import line `import { initDiscoveryQuestBridge } from './systems/questSystem.js';` and 1 invocation `initDiscoveryQuestBridge();` immediately after the existing `if (import.meta.env.DEV) { ... }` block. Not DEV-gated because this is gameplay wiring, not dev-tooling. Documented inline.
synthesis_evidence.build_validation: npm run build succeeded (12.01s) — no resolution errors, no warnings introduced by this work. Static-import chain GameContainer.jsx → questSystem.js → discoveryUnlocks.js + discoverySystem.js resolves cleanly.

spec_discrepancy_resolution.issue: Spec example showed `unlockQuests: [...]` plural; runtimeAudit reads `entry.quest || entry.questId` singular.
spec_discrepancy_resolution.decision: Worker chose singular `questId` field. Aligns with audit's existing read pattern. If multi-quest unlocks become needed, future cycle can extend to `questIds: [...]` and update audit in same change.

audit_keying_mismatch_known.issue: runtimeAudit.auditDiscoveryUnlocks() validates DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS keys against `regions.js` region IDs (arizona, andes, arabian, etc.). Worker chose to key DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS by WORLD_LOCATIONS location IDs (desert_foraging, copper_mine, salt_river, mountain_range) — which is the correct granularity for per-location discovery events fired by WorldMapScene.revealNode.
audit_keying_mismatch_known.consequence: On next dev boot, runtimeAudit will report 4 errors of the form `DISCOVERY_UNLOCKS entry "<locId>" does not match any region in regions.js`.
audit_keying_mismatch_known.mitigation: Worker explicitly documented this in discoveryUnlocks.js header. Recommend follow-up cycle to update auditDiscoveryUnlocks to validate against worldMapData.js location IDs OR support both keying schemes.
audit_keying_mismatch_known.severity_classification: MEDIUM — runtime audit logs to console.error but never blocks boot. Real gameplay wiring still works correctly.

quest_thematic_matches.desert_foraging: desert_healer — Mrs. Ramirez's medicinal plant ecology quest. Strong theme match.
quest_thematic_matches.copper_mine: bridge_collapse — materials/copper-ore quest. Strong theme match (worker explicitly noted the quest 'uses copper ore mining').
quest_thematic_matches.salt_river: boat_puzzle — buoyancy/Archimedes' Principle. Strong theme match. Worker noted `the_living_fluid` is also adjacent (non-Newtonian fluid) but boat_puzzle is the canonical fluid-physics intro.
quest_thematic_matches.mountain_range: PENDING — no force/gravity/incline intro quest exists. `one_sided_forest` (Möbius cave) is topology, not physics. Worker correctly left as pending:true with TODO.

notes.queue_based_design_rationale: Discovery events fire from WorldMapScene mid-render (specifically from revealNode after revealArea + redrawFog). Calling startQuest() directly there would interrupt the player with a quest-accept dialog at an unnatural moment. The queue-based design lets scenes consume the queue at deliberate checkpoints (NPC dialog, scene transition) for better UX. Tradeoff: requires future scene-side integration to call consumePendingDiscoveryUnlocks(state) — a follow-up wiring task, not a blocker. The queue is in-memory only (not persisted) so unconsumed unlocks reset on page reload — which is fine because the player can always trigger the same discovery again next session.
